Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主命|12=draft}}, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja|温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kurobane Onsen|ja|黒羽温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja|温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasushiobara|ja|那須塩原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Nasu Onsen Shrine|ja|那須温泉神社|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasu District|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu|ja|那須町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja|温泉神社 (日光市)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kinugawa Onsen|ja|鬼怒川温泉|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yumae Shrine|ja|湯前神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Atami|ja|熱海市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Izuyama Shrine|ja|射山神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakakibara Onsen|ja|榊原温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|ja|湯泉神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kobe)|ja|北区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Arima Onsen|ja|有馬温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Sometimes referred to as "Onsen Shrine." Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and Kumano-Kusumi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|12=draft}} (Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Toyooka|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Yuyama-no-Kami (湯山主神) and others. It is said that Dōchi, who visited this shrine, was divinely inspired to open the public bath "Mandarayu." * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|温泉神社 (白浜町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District|ja|西牟婁郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|ja|南紀白浜温泉|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yumoto Shrine|ja|湯本神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Maniwa|ja|真庭市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}}) - The location was formerly called Yubara, but upon Maniwa City's establishment, it was renamed Yubara Onsen. * {{ill|Miyu Shrine|ja|御湯神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwami District|ja|岩美郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwami|ja|岩美町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwai Onsen|ja|岩井温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Yakami-hime-no-Mikoto, Miino-Kami, and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Shrine|ja|玉作湯神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Kushiakarutama-no-Kami, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|ja|湯神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Higashidōgo Shrine|ja|東道後神社|12=draft}} (Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama, {{ill|Higashidōgo Onsen|ja|東道後温泉郷|12=draft}}) - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|12=draft}}. Enshrines Amenominakanushi-no-Kami, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, and Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Beppu|ja|別府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Beppu Onsen|ja|別府温泉|12=draft}}) - Founded during the Taishō period. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. Currently merged into {{ill|Hachiman Asami Shrine|ja|八幡朝見神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|12=draft}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, {{ill|Tetsuwana Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hinokuma Hime Shrine|ja|火男火売神社|12=draft}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, Tetsuwana Onsen) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Hinokagutsuchi-no-Mikoto and Hiyakehayahime-no-Mikoto. It is said that {{ill|Ippen|ja|一遍|12=draft}} Shōnin was inspired by this shrine, called Tsurumi Gongen, to create a stone steam bath (mushi-yu). "Hayami no Yu," a hot spring flowing from the base of the sacred Tsurumidake mountain, is believed to have been directed to Dōgo Onsen by Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto through an underwater channel. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unzen|ja|雲仙市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Obama Onsen|ja|小浜町雲仙|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unzen Onsen|ja|雲仙温泉|12=draft}}) - A Kokusai-mizai-sha. Dedicated to the deity of Tsukushi Island (modern {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州|12=draft}}), represented in the form of "Isshin Shimen" (a single body with four faces). The shrine is also known as "Shimen-gū." The name "Unzen" derives from the reading of 温泉 (onsen), but the shrine is pronounced as written: "Onsen Jinja." Over a dozen branch shrines of the same name exist within the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|ja|島原半島|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja|温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Shimabara|ja|島原市|12=draft}}, Kamenokō-chō) * {{ill|Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja|温泉熊野神社|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Shimabara) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Kunimi-machi Tabirachō) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Azuma-machi Fuenami) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Chijiwa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Minamishimabara|ja|南島原市|12=draft}}, Ariake-machi Yamakawa) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Minamishimabara, Kazusa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja|温泉神社 (八代市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yatsushiro|ja|八代市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hinagu Onsen|ja|日奈久温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|Onsen Temple|ja|温泉寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|温泉|" to "|温泉|1=Onsen|"
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|'''Overview of Shrines Related to Hot Springs'''|'''Individual Shrines Named Onsen Shrine'''|温泉神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} '''Onsen Shrine''' (温泉神社, Onsen Jinja) refers to a type of {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}}, or a commonly used name, associated with {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|12=draft}} (hot springs).
== Overview == An Onsen Shrine is a shrine connected to or dedicated to hot springs. Such shrines are found across Japan, primarily in hot spring areas.
Japan's landscape, intersected by volcanic zones, is abundant in hot springs. Since ancient times, hot springs have been revered as the abodes of deities, known as "Hot Spring Deities" or "Onsen Kami." Shrines dedicated to these deities are referred to as "Yugami Shrines" or "Onsen Shrines." Many ancient hot springs are traditionally associated with deities such as {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|12=draft}}, who are often enshrined as Onsen Kami.
According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, there are three shrines specifically named "Onsen Shrine," along with others such as "Onsen Stone Shrine," "Hot Spring Shrine," "Yugami Shrine," "Miyu Shrine," and "Tamakusuyugami Shrine," making a total of ten shrines related to hot springs listed as {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}.
== Shrines Related to Hot Springs Across Japan ==
* {{ill|Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja|温泉石神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawatabi Onsen|ja|川渡温泉|12=draft}}) - {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja|温泉神社 (大崎市)|12=draft}} (Miyagi Prefecture, Ōsaki, {{ill|Naruko Onsen|ja|鳴子温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yuzusame Shrine|ja|由豆佐売神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka|ja|鶴岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yutagawa Onsen|ja|湯田川温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Mizokuihime-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|湯泉神社 (郡山市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Bandai-Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, among others. Divinity of hot springs brought from Izu by the {{ill|Azumi Itō Clan|ja|安積伊東氏|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|12=draft}} (Fukushima Prefecture, {{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主命|12=draft}}, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja|温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kurobane Onsen|ja|黒羽温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja|温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasushiobara|ja|那須塩原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Nasu Onsen Shrine|ja|那須温泉神社|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasu District|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu|ja|那須町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Famous for the {{ill|Sessho-seki|ja|殺生石|12=draft}}. The "Nasu Yumoto Daijingū" mentioned in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft}} is said to refer to this shrine. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja|温泉神社 (日光市)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kinugawa Onsen|ja|鬼怒川温泉|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yumae Shrine|ja|湯前神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Atami|ja|熱海市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Izuyama Shrine|ja|射山神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakakibara Onsen|ja|榊原温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|ja|湯泉神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kobe)|ja|北区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Arima Onsen|ja|有馬温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Sometimes referred to as "Onsen Shrine." Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and Kumano-Kusumi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|12=draft}} (Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Toyooka|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Yuyama-no-Kami (湯山主神) and others. It is said that Dōchi, who visited this shrine, was divinely inspired to open the public bath "Mandarayu." * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|温泉神社 (白浜町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District|ja|西牟婁郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|ja|南紀白浜温泉|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yumoto Shrine|ja|湯本神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Maniwa|ja|真庭市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}}) - The location was formerly called Yubara, but upon Maniwa City's establishment, it was renamed Yubara Onsen. * {{ill|Miyu Shrine|ja|御湯神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwami District|ja|岩美郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwami|ja|岩美町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwai Onsen|ja|岩井温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Yakami-hime-no-Mikoto, Miino-Kami, and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Shrine|ja|玉作湯神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Kushiakarutama-no-Kami, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|ja|湯神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Higashidōgo Shrine|ja|東道後神社|12=draft}} (Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama, {{ill|Higashidōgo Onsen|ja|東道後温泉郷|12=draft}}) - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|12=draft}}. Enshrines Amenominakanushi-no-Kami, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, and Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Beppu|ja|別府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Beppu Onsen|ja|別府温泉|12=draft}}) - Founded during the Taishō period. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. Currently merged into {{ill|Hachiman Asami Shrine|ja|八幡朝見神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|12=draft}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, {{ill|Tetsuwana Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hinokuma Hime Shrine|ja|火男火売神社|12=draft}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, Tetsuwana Onsen) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Hinokagutsuchi-no-Mikoto and Hiyakehayahime-no-Mikoto. It is said that {{ill|Ippen|ja|一遍|12=draft}} Shōnin was inspired by this shrine, called Tsurumi Gongen, to create a stone steam bath (mushi-yu). "Hayami no Yu," a hot spring flowing from the base of the sacred Tsurumidake mountain, is believed to have been directed to Dōgo Onsen by Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto through an underwater channel. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unzen|ja|雲仙市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Obama Onsen|ja|小浜町雲仙|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unzen Onsen|ja|雲仙温泉|12=draft}}) - A Kokusai-mizai-sha. Dedicated to the deity of Tsukushi Island (modern {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州|12=draft}}), represented in the form of "Isshin Shimen" (a single body with four faces). The shrine is also known as "Shimen-gū." The name "Unzen" derives from the reading of 温泉 (onsen), but the shrine is pronounced as written: "Onsen Jinja." Over a dozen branch shrines of the same name exist within the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|ja|島原半島|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja|温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Shimabara|ja|島原市|12=draft}}, Kamenokō-chō) * {{ill|Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja|温泉熊野神社|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Shimabara) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Kunimi-machi Tabirachō) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Azuma-machi Fuenami) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Chijiwa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Minamishimabara|ja|南島原市|12=draft}}, Ariake-machi Yamakawa) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Minamishimabara, Kazusa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja|温泉神社 (八代市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yatsushiro|ja|八代市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hinagu Onsen|ja|日奈久温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|Onsen Temple|ja|温泉寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=draft}}

Shrines dedicated to these deities are referred to as "Yugami Shrines" or "Onsen Shrines." Many ancient hot springs are traditionally associated with deities such as {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|12=draft}}, who are often enshrined as Onsen Kami.

* {{ill|Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja|温泉石神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawatabi Onsen|ja|川渡温泉|12=draft}}) - {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja|温泉神社 (大崎市)|12=draft}} (Miyagi Prefecture, Ōsaki, {{ill|Naruko Onsen|ja|鳴子温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yuzusame Shrine|ja|由豆佐売神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka|ja|鶴岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yutagawa Onsen|ja|湯田川温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Mizokuihime-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|湯泉神社 (郡山市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Bandai-Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, among others. Divinity of hot springs brought from Izu by the {{ill|Azumi Itō Clan|ja|安積伊東氏|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|12=draft}} (Fukushima Prefecture, {{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主命|12=draft}}, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja|温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kurobane Onsen|ja|黒羽温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja|温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasushiobara|ja|那須塩原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Nasu Onsen Shrine|ja|那須温泉神社|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasu District|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu|ja|那須町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Famous for the {{ill|Sessho-seki|ja|殺生石|12=draft}}. The "Nasu Yumoto Daijingū" mentioned in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft}} is said to refer to this shrine. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja|温泉神社 (日光市)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kinugawa Onsen|ja|鬼怒川温泉|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yumae Shrine|ja|湯前神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Atami|ja|熱海市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Atami Onsen|ja|熱海温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Izuyama Shrine|ja|射山神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakakibara Onsen|ja|榊原温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|ja|湯泉神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kobe)|ja|北区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Arima Onsen|ja|有馬温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Sometimes referred to as "Onsen Shrine." Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and Kumano-Kusumi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|12=draft}} (Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Toyooka|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Yuyama-no-Kami (湯山主神) and others. It is said that Dōchi, who visited this shrine, was divinely inspired to open the public bath "Mandarayu." * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|温泉神社 (白浜町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District|ja|西牟婁郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|ja|南紀白浜温泉|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yumoto Shrine|ja|湯本神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Maniwa|ja|真庭市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}}) - The location was formerly called Yubara, but upon Maniwa City's establishment, it was renamed Yubara Onsen. * {{ill|Miyu Shrine|ja|御湯神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwami District|ja|岩美郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwami|ja|岩美町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwai Onsen|ja|岩井温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Yakami-hime-no-Mikoto, Miino-Kami, and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Shrine|ja|玉作湯神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Kushiakarutama-no-Kami, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|ja|湯神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Higashidōgo Shrine|ja|東道後神社|12=draft}} (Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama, {{ill|Higashidōgo Onsen|ja|東道後温泉郷|12=draft}}) - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|12=draft}}. Enshrines Amenominakanushi-no-Kami, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, and Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Beppu|ja|別府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Beppu Onsen|ja|別府温泉|12=draft}}) - Founded during the Taishō period. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. Currently merged into {{ill|Hachiman Asami Shrine|ja|八幡朝見神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|12=draft}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, {{ill|Tetsuwana Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hinokuma Hime Shrine|ja|火男火売神社|12=draft}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, Tetsuwana Onsen) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Hinokagutsuchi-no-Mikoto and Hiyakehayahime-no-Mikoto. It is said that {{ill|Ippen|ja|一遍|12=draft}} Shōnin was inspired by this shrine, called Tsurumi Gongen, to create a stone steam bath (mushi-yu). "Hayami no Yu," a hot spring flowing from the base of the sacred Tsurumidake mountain, is believed to have been directed to Dōgo Onsen by Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto through an underwater channel. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unzen|ja|雲仙市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Obama Onsen|ja|小浜町雲仙|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unzen Onsen|ja|雲仙温泉|12=draft}}) - A Kokusai-mizai-sha. Dedicated to the deity of Tsukushi Island (modern {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州|12=draft}}), represented in the form of "Isshin Shimen" (a single body with four faces). The shrine is also known as "Shimen-gū." The name "Unzen" derives from the reading of 温泉 (onsen), but the shrine is pronounced as written: "Onsen Jinja." Over a dozen branch shrines of the same name exist within the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|ja|島原半島|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja|温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Shimabara|ja|島原市|12=draft}}, Kamenokō-chō) * {{ill|Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja|温泉熊野神社|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Shimabara) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Kunimi-machi Tabirachō) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Azuma-machi Fuenami) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Chijiwa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Minamishimabara|ja|南島原市|12=draft}}, Ariake-machi Yamakawa) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Minamishimabara, Kazusa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja|温泉神社 (八代市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yatsushiro|ja|八代市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hinagu Onsen|ja|日奈久温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|Onsen Temple|ja|温泉寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|温泉|" to "|温泉|1=Onsen|"
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|'''Overview of Shrines Related to Hot Springs'''|'''Individual Shrines Named Onsen Shrine'''|温泉神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} '''Onsen Shrine''' (温泉神社, Onsen Jinja) refers to a type of {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}}, or a commonly used name, associated with {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft}} (hot springs).
== Overview == An Onsen Shrine is a shrine connected to or dedicated to hot springs. Such shrines are found across Japan, primarily in hot spring areas.
Japan's landscape, intersected by volcanic zones, is abundant in hot springs. Since ancient times, hot springs have been revered as the abodes of deities, known as "Hot Spring Deities" or "Onsen Kami." Shrines dedicated to these deities are referred to as "Yugami Shrines" or "Onsen Shrines." Many ancient hot springs are traditionally associated with deities such as {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|12=draft}}, who are often enshrined as Onsen Kami.
According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, there are three shrines specifically named "Onsen Shrine," along with others such as "Onsen Stone Shrine," "Hot Spring Shrine," "Yugami Shrine," "Miyu Shrine," and "Tamakusuyugami Shrine," making a total of ten shrines related to hot springs listed as {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}.
== Shrines Related to Hot Springs Across Japan ==
* {{ill|Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja|温泉石神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawatabi Onsen|ja|川渡温泉|12=draft}}) - {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja|温泉神社 (大崎市)|12=draft}} (Miyagi Prefecture, Ōsaki, {{ill|Naruko Onsen|ja|鳴子温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yuzusame Shrine|ja|由豆佐売神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka|ja|鶴岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yutagawa Onsen|ja|湯田川温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Mizokuihime-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|湯泉神社 (郡山市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Bandai-Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, among others. Divinity of hot springs brought from Izu by the {{ill|Azumi Itō Clan|ja|安積伊東氏|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|12=draft}} (Fukushima Prefecture, {{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主命|12=draft}}, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja|温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kurobane Onsen|ja|黒羽温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja|温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasushiobara|ja|那須塩原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Nasu Onsen Shrine|ja|那須温泉神社|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasu District|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu|ja|那須町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Famous for the {{ill|Sessho-seki|ja|殺生石|12=draft}}. The "Nasu Yumoto Daijingū" mentioned in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft}} is said to refer to this shrine. It is said that Dōchi, who visited this shrine, was divinely inspired to open the public bath "Mandarayu." * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|温泉神社 (白浜町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District|ja|西牟婁郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|ja|南紀白浜温泉|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yumoto Shrine|ja|湯本神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Maniwa|ja|真庭市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}}) - The location was formerly called Yubara, but upon Maniwa City's establishment, it was renamed Yubara Onsen. * {{ill|Miyu Shrine|ja|御湯神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwami District|ja|岩美郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwami|ja|岩美町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwai Onsen|ja|岩井温泉|12=draft}}) - Shikinai-sha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Higashidōgo Shrine|ja|東道後神社|12=draft}} (Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama, {{ill|Higashidōgo Onsen|ja|東道後温泉郷|12=draft}}) - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|12=draft}}. Enshrines Amenominakanushi-no-Kami, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, and Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Beppu|ja|別府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Beppu Onsen|ja|別府温泉|12=draft}}) - Founded during the Taishō period. Dedicated to the deity of Tsukushi Island (modern {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州|12=draft}}), represented in the form of "Isshin Shimen" (a single body with four faces). The shrine is also known as "Shimen-gū." The name "Unzen" derives from the reading of 温泉 (onsen), but the shrine is pronounced as written: "Onsen Jinja." Over a dozen branch shrines of the same name exist within the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|ja|島原半島|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja|温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Shimabara|ja|島原市|12=draft}}, Kamenokō-chō) * {{ill|Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja|温泉熊野神社|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Shimabara) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Kunimi-machi Tabirachō) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Azuma-machi Fuenami) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Chijiwa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Minamishimabara|ja|南島原市|12=draft}}, Ariake-machi Yamakawa) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|12=draft}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Minamishimabara, Kazusa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja|温泉神社 (八代市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yatsushiro|ja|八代市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hinagu Onsen|ja|日奈久温泉|12=draft}}) - Enshrines Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|Onsen Temple|ja|温泉寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=draft}}
{{citation needed|Onsen Jinja}} <!--Onsen Shrines-->
{{Onsen}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|温泉に関わる神社の総論|温泉という社名を持つ個別の神社|温泉神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} '''温泉神社'''（おんせんじんじゃ）は、[[温泉]]に関わる、あるいは温泉を祀る[[神社]]。日本各地の主に温泉地に存在する。
==概要== 日本は火山帯が縦横に走り、各地に温泉がある。温泉は、湯神・温泉神として古来より崇敬の対象となってきた。その神を祀るのが湯神社・温泉神社である。古代に発見された温泉の多くは、大己貴神（[[大国主]]）と少彦名神（[[スクナビコナ]]）が発見したと伝えられ、温泉神社の祭神である温泉神にはこの二神が充てられていることが多い。
[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載されている神社は、｢温泉神社」が3社、｢温泉石神社」｢湯泉神社」｢湯神社」｢御湯神社」｢玉作湯神社」が各1社など、温泉に関する[[延喜式神名帳#式内社|式内社]]は計10社となっている。
== 全国の温泉に関する神社 == * [[温泉石神社]] （[[宮城県]][[大崎市]]鳴子温泉川渡・[[川渡温泉]]） - [[式内社]]。祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命。 * [[温泉神社 (大崎市)|温泉神社]] （宮城県大崎市鳴子温泉湯元・[[鳴子温泉]]） - 式内社。祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命。 * [[酢川温泉神社]] （[[山形県]][[山形市]]蔵王温泉・[[蔵王温泉]]） * [[由豆佐売神社]] （山形県[[鶴岡市]]湯田川・[[湯田川温泉]]） - 式内社。祭神は[[溝樴姫命]]・大己貴命・少彦名命。 * [[湯泉神社 (郡山市)|湯泉神社]] （[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[熱海町熱海]]・[[磐梯熱海温泉]]） - 祭神は少彦名命など。[[伊東氏#安積伊東氏|安積伊東氏]]が伊豆から温泉神の分霊を勧請。 * [[温泉神社 (いわき市)|温泉神社]] （福島県[[いわき市]][[常磐湯本町]]・[[いわき湯本温泉]]） - 式内社。祭神は大己貴命・[[事代主|事代主命]]・少彦名命。 * [[温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|温泉神社]] （[[栃木県]][[大田原市]]中野内） - 祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命ほか。 * [[温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|温泉神社]] （栃木県[[那須塩原市]]湯本塩原・[[塩原新湯温泉]]、[[塩原元湯温泉]]） - 祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命。 * [[那須温泉神社]] （栃木県[[那須郡]][[那須町]]湯本・[[那須湯本温泉#那須湯本温泉|那須湯本温泉]]） - 式内社。[[殺生石]]で有名。[[平家物語]]における[[那須与一]]の台詞に登場する「那須湯前大明神」はこの神社であるという説もある。祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命。 * [[温泉神社(日光市湯元)|温泉神社]] （栃木県[[日光市]]湯元・[[日光湯元温泉]]） * [[鬼怒川温泉神社]] （栃木県日光市鬼怒川温泉滝・[[鬼怒川温泉]]） * [[湯前神社]] （[[静岡県]][[熱海市]]上宿町・[[熱海温泉]]） - 式内社。祭神は少彦名命。 * [[射山神社]] （[[三重県]][[津市]]榊原町・[[榊原温泉]]）- 式内社。祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命。 * [[湯泉神社]] （[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[北区 (神戸市)|北区]][[有馬町]]・[[有馬温泉]]） - 式内社。「温泉神社」と表記されることもある。祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命・熊野久須美命。 * [[四所神社 (豊岡市)|四所神社]] （兵庫県[[豊岡市]]城崎町・[[城崎温泉]]）- 祭神は湯山主神（ゆやまぬしのかみ）ほか。当社に参拝した道智に神託があり、外湯「まんだら湯」を開湯したと伝わる。 * [[温泉神社 (白浜町)|温泉神社]] （[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[白浜町]]・[[南紀白浜温泉]]） * [[湯本神社]] （[[岡山県]][[真庭市]]湯原温泉・[[湯原温泉]]） - 所在地大字も湯本であったが、真庭市新設時に湯原温泉へ改称した。 * [[岩井温泉#御湯神社|御湯神社]] （[[鳥取県]][[岩美郡]][[岩美町]]岩井・[[岩井温泉]]）- 式内社。祭神は大己貴命・八上姫命・御井神・猿田彦命。 * [[玉作湯神社]] （[[島根県]][[松江市]]玉造・[[玉造温泉]]） - 式内社。祭神は[[櫛明玉神]]・大名持神・少毘古那神。 * [[湯神社]] （[[愛媛県]][[松山市]][[道後湯之町 (松山市)|道後湯之町]]・[[道後温泉]]） - 式内社。祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命。 * [[東道後神社]] （愛媛県松山市[[南久米町]]・[[東道後温泉郷|東道後温泉]]） - [[日尾八幡神社]]の境内社。祭神は[[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]、[[天照大神|天照皇大神]]、[[ツクヨミ|月夜見大神]]。 * [[温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|温泉神社]] （[[大分県]][[別府市]]青山町・[[別府温泉]]） - 大正時代の創始。祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命ほか。現在は[[八幡朝見神社]]に合祀。 * [[温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|温泉神社]] （大分県別府市鉄輪・[[鉄輪温泉]]） * [[火男火売神社]] （大分県別府市東山・鉄輪温泉） - 式内社。祭神は[[火之加具土命]]、火焼速女命。[[一遍]]上人が鶴見権現と呼ばれる当社の導きにより鉄輪温泉の石風呂（蒸し湯）を開いたという。また、御神体である[[鶴見岳]]の麓から湧く「[[速見の湯]]」は、大国主命が海底に管を通して[[道後温泉]]へと導いたとされている。 * [[温泉神社 (雲仙市)|温泉神社]] （[[長崎県]][[雲仙市]]小浜町・[[雲仙温泉]]） - 国史見在社。一身四面の姿で表現される筑紫島（現在の[[九州]]）の神を祀ることから、別名を四面宮という。雲仙岳はかつて温泉と書いて「うんぜん」と読ませていたが、温泉神社については表記どおり「おんせんじんじゃ」と呼ばれている。[[島原半島]]内に十数社の同名の分社がある。 * [[温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町) |温泉神社]] （長崎県[[島原市]]亀の甲町） * [[温泉熊野神社]] （長崎県島原市杉山町） * [[温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|温泉神社]] （長崎県雲仙市国見町多比良丁） * [[温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|温泉神社]] （長崎県雲仙市吾妻町） * [[温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|温泉神社]] （長崎県雲仙市千々石町） * [[温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|温泉神社]] （長崎県[[南島原市]]有家町） * [[温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|温泉神社]] （長崎県南島原市加津佐町） * [[温泉神社 (八代市)|温泉神社]] （[[熊本県]][[八代市]]日奈久・[[日奈久温泉]]） - 祭神は[[市杵島姫命]]。
== 関連事項 == *[[温泉]] *[[温泉神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] *[[温泉寺]] *[[開湯伝説]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おんせんしんしや}} [[Category:温泉神社|*]]

祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命ほか。 * [[温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|温泉神社]] （栃木県[[那須塩原市]]湯本塩原・[[塩原新湯温泉]]、[[塩原元湯温泉]]） - 祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命。 * [[那須温泉神社]] （栃木県[[那須郡]][[那須町]]湯本・[[那須湯本温泉#那須湯本温泉|那須湯本温泉]]） - 式内社。[[殺生石]]で有名。[[平家物語]]における[[那須与一]]の台詞に登場する「那須湯前大明神」はこの神社であるという説もある。祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命。 * [[温泉神社(日光市湯元)|温泉神社]] （栃木県[[日光市]]湯元・[[日光湯元温泉]]） * [[鬼怒川温泉神社]] （栃木県日光市鬼怒川温泉滝・[[鬼怒川温泉]]） * [[湯前神社]] （[[静岡県]][[熱海市]]上宿町・[[熱海温泉]]） - 所在地大字も湯本であったが、真庭市新設時に湯原温泉へ改称した。 * [[岩井温泉#御湯神社|御湯神社]] （[[鳥取県]][[岩美郡]][[岩美町]]岩井・[[岩井温泉]]）- 式内社。祭神は大己貴命・八上姫命・御井神・猿田彦命。 * [[玉作湯神社]] （[[島根県]][[松江市]]玉造・[[玉造温泉]]） - 式内社。祭神は[[櫛明玉神]]・大名持神・少毘古那神。 * [[湯神社]] （[[愛媛県]][[松山市]][[道後湯之町 (松山市)|道後湯之町]]・[[道後温泉]]） - 式内社。祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命。 * [[東道後神社]] （愛媛県松山市[[南久米町]]・[[東道後温泉郷|東道後温泉]]） - [[日尾八幡神社]]の境内社。祭神は[[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]、[[天照大神|天照皇大神]]、[[ツクヨミ|月夜見大神]]。 * [[温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|温泉神社]] （[[大分県]][[別府市]]青山町・[[別府温泉]]） - 大正時代の創始。祭神は大己貴命・少彦名命ほか。現在は[[八幡朝見神社]]に合祀。 * [[温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|温泉神社]] （大分県別府市鉄輪・[[鉄輪温泉]]） * [[火男火売神社]] （大分県別府市東山・鉄輪温泉） - 式内社。祭神は[[火之加具土命]]、火焼速女命。[[一遍]]上人が鶴見権現と呼ばれる当社の導きにより鉄輪温泉の石風呂（蒸し湯）を開いたという。また、御神体である[[鶴見岳]]の麓から湧く「[[速見の湯]]」は、大国主命が海底に管を通して[[道後温泉]]へと導いたとされている。 * [[温泉神社 (雲仙市)|温泉神社]] （[[長崎県]][[雲仙市]]小浜町・[[雲仙温泉]]） - 国史見在社。一身四面の姿で表現される筑紫島（現在の[[九州]]）の神を祀ることから、別名を四面宮という。雲仙岳はかつて温泉と書いて「うんぜん」と読ませていたが、温泉神社については表記どおり「おんせんじんじゃ」と呼ばれている。[[島原半島]]内に十数社の同名の分社がある。 * [[温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町) |温泉神社]] （長崎県[[島原市]]亀の甲町） * [[温泉熊野神社]] （長崎県島原市杉山町） * [[温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|温泉神社]] （長崎県雲仙市国見町多比良丁） * [[温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|温泉神社]] （長崎県雲仙市吾妻町） * [[温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|温泉神社]] （長崎県雲仙市千々石町） * [[温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|温泉神社]] （長崎県[[南島原市]]有家町） * [[温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|温泉神社]] （長崎県南島原市加津佐町） * [[温泉神社 (八代市)|温泉神社]] （[[熊本県]][[八代市]]日奈久・[[日奈久温泉]]） - 祭神は[[市杵島姫命]]。
== 関連事項 == *[[温泉]] *[[温泉神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] *[[温泉寺]] *[[開湯伝説]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おんせんしんしや}} [[Category:温泉神社|*]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11562963]] Wikidata name: onsen shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Onsen Shrine (disambiguation)}} '''Onsen Shrine''' (温泉神社, Onsen Jinja) refers to a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]], or a commonly used name, associated with [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] (hot springs).
== Overview == An Onsen Shrine is a shrine connected to or dedicated to hot springs. Such shrines are found across Japan, primarily in hot spring areas.
Japan's landscape, intersected by volcanic zones, is abundant in hot springs. Since ancient times, hot springs have been revered as the abodes of deities, known as "Hot Spring Deities" or "Onsen Kami." Shrines dedicated to these deities are referred to as "Yugami Shrines" or "Onsen Shrines." Many ancient hot springs are traditionally associated with deities such as [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] and {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, who are often enshrined as Onsen Kami.
According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, there are three shrines specifically named "Onsen Shrine," along with others such as "Onsen Stone Shrine," "Hot Spring Shrine," "Yugami Shrine," "Miyu Shrine," and "Tamakusuyugami Shrine," making a total of ten shrines related to hot springs listed as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}.
== Shrines Related to Hot Springs Across Japan ==
* {{ill|Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja|温泉石神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Ishi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]], {{ill|Kawatabi Onsen|ja|川渡温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawatabi Onsen|qq=|1=Kawatabi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478932}}) - {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja|温泉神社 (大崎市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture, Ōsaki, {{ill|Naruko Onsen|ja|鳴子温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruko Onsen|qq=|1=Naruko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675321}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yuzusame Shrine|ja|由豆佐売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzusame Shrine|qq=|1=Yuzusame Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577650}} ([[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], {{ill|Yutagawa Onsen|ja|湯田川温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutagawa Onsen|qq=|1=Yutagawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563759}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Mizokuihime-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|湯泉神社 (郡山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōriyama|Kōriyama]], {{ill|Bandai-Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandai-Atami Onsen|qq=|1=Bandai Atami Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587893}}) - Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, among others. Divinity of hot springs brought from Izu by the {{ill|Azumi Itō Clan|ja|安積伊東氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Itō Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]], [[:en:Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja|温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], {{ill|Kurobane Onsen|ja|黒羽温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurobane Onsen|qq=|1=Kurobane Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678785}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja|温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara]], {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|qq=|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Nasu Onsen Shrine|ja|那須温泉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen Shrine|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District]], [[:en:Nasu, Tochigi|Nasu]], {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|qq=|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}}) - Shikinaisha. Famous for the [[:en:Sessho-seki|Sessho-seki]]. The "Nasu Yumoto Daijingū" mentioned in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]] is said to refer to this shrine. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja|温泉神社 (日光市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], [[:en:Kinugawa Onsen|Kinugawa Onsen]]) * {{ill|Yumae Shrine|ja|湯前神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumae Shrine|qq=|1=Yuzen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563474}} ([[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Atami|Atami]], [[:en:Atami Onsen|Atami Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Izuyama Shrine|ja|射山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuyama Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]], {{ill|Sakakibara Onsen|ja|榊原温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakibara Onsen|qq=|1=Sakakibara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541719}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|ja|湯泉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|qq=|1=Yusen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563671}} ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Kobe)]], [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Sometimes referred to as "Onsen Shrine." Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and Kumano-Kusumi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture, [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka]], {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}) - Enshrines Yuyama-no-Kami (湯山主神) and others. It is said that Dōchi, who visited this shrine, was divinely inspired to open the public bath "Mandarayu." * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|温泉神社 (白浜町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District]], [[:en:Shirahama, Wakayama|Shirahama]], [[:en:Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|Nanki Shirahama Onsen]]) * {{ill|Yumoto Shrine|ja|湯本神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Maniwa|Maniwa]], {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}) - The location was formerly called Yubara, but upon Maniwa City's establishment, it was renamed Yubara Onsen. * {{ill|Miyu Shrine|ja|御湯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwami District, Tottori|Iwami District]], [[:en:Iwami, Tottori|Iwami]], {{ill|Iwai Onsen|ja|岩井温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Onsen|qq=|1=Iwai Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474068}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Yakami-hime-no-Mikoto, Miino-Kami, and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Shrine|ja|玉作湯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukuri Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Tamatsukuri Onsen|Tamatsukuri Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Kushiakarutama-no-Kami, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|ja|湯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Higashidōgo Shrine|ja|東道後神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日尾八幡神社}} (Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama, {{ill|Higashidōgo Onsen|ja|東道後温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Onsen|qq=|1=Higashidōgo Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528419}}) - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hio Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Hio Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505266}}. Enshrines Amenominakanushi-no-Kami, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, and Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]]) - Founded during the Taishō period. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. Currently merged into {{ill|Hachiman Asami Shrine|ja|八幡朝見神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Asami Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, {{ill|Tetsuwana Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuwana Onsen|qq=|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}}) * {{ill|Hinokuma Hime Shrine|ja|火男火売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokuma Hime Shrine|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, Tetsuwana Onsen) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Hinokagutsuchi-no-Mikoto and Hiyakehayahime-no-Mikoto. It is said that [[:en:Ippen|Ippen]] Shōnin was inspired by this shrine, called Tsurumi Gongen, to create a stone steam bath (mushi-yu). "Hayami no Yu," a hot spring flowing from the base of the sacred Tsurumidake mountain, is believed to have been directed to Dōgo Onsen by Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto through an underwater channel. * {{ill|Unzen Onsen Shrine|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unzen Onsen Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Unzen, Nagasaki|Unzen]], {{ill|Obama Onsen|ja|小浜町雲仙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obama Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Unzen Onsen|Unzen Onsen]]) - A Kokusai-mizai-sha. Dedicated to the deity of Tsukushi Island (modern [[:en:Kyushu|Kyūshū]]), represented in the form of "Isshin Shimen" (a single body with four faces). The shrine is also known as "Shimen-gū." The name "Unzen" derives from the reading of 温泉 (onsen), but the shrine is pronounced as written: "Onsen Jinja." Over a dozen branch shrines of the same name exist within the [[:en:Shimabara Peninsula|Shimabara Peninsula]]. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja|温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, [[:en:Shimabara, Nagasaki|Shimabara]], Kamenokō-chō) * {{ill|Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja|温泉熊野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Kumano Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Shimabara) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Kunimi-machi Tabirachō) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Azuma-machi Fuenami) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Chijiwa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, [[:en:Minamishimabara|Minamishimabara]], Ariake-machi Yamakawa) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11562963]] Wikidata name: onsen shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Onsen Shrine (disambiguation)}} '''Onsen Shrine''' (温泉神社, Onsen Jinja) refers to a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]], or a commonly used name, associated with [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] (hot springs).
== Overview == An Onsen Shrine is a shrine connected to or dedicated to hot springs. Such shrines are found across Japan, primarily in hot spring areas.
Japan's landscape, intersected by volcanic zones, is abundant in hot springs. Since ancient times, hot springs have been revered as the abodes of deities, known as "Hot Spring Deities" or "Onsen Kami." Shrines dedicated to these deities are referred to as "Yugami Shrines" or "Onsen Shrines." Many ancient hot springs are traditionally associated with deities such as [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] and {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, who are often enshrined as Onsen Kami.
According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, there are three shrines specifically named "Onsen Shrine," along with others such as "Onsen Stone Shrine," "Hot Spring Shrine," "Yugami Shrine," "Miyu Shrine," and "Tamakusuyugami Shrine," making a total of ten shrines related to hot springs listed as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}.
== Shrines Related to Hot Springs Across Japan ==
* {{ill|Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja|温泉石神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Ishi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]], {{ill|Kawatabi Onsen|ja|川渡温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawatabi Onsen|qq=|1=Kawatabi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478932}}) - {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja|温泉神社 (大崎市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture, Ōsaki, {{ill|Naruko Onsen|ja|鳴子温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruko Onsen|qq=|1=Naruko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675321}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yuzusame Shrine|ja|由豆佐売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzusame Shrine|qq=|1=Yuzusame Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577650}} ([[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], {{ill|Yutagawa Onsen|ja|湯田川温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutagawa Onsen|qq=|1=Yutagawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563759}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Mizokuihime-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|湯泉神社 (郡山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōriyama|Kōriyama]], {{ill|Bandai-Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandai-Atami Onsen|qq=|1=Bandai Atami Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587893}}) - Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, among others. Divinity of hot springs brought from Izu by the {{ill|Azumi Itō Clan|ja|安積伊東氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Itō Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]], [[:en:Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja|温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], {{ill|Kurobane Onsen|ja|黒羽温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurobane Onsen|qq=|1=Kurobane Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678785}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja|温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara]], {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|qq=|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Nasu Onsen Shrine|ja|那須温泉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen Shrine|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District]], [[:en:Nasu, Tochigi|Nasu]], {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|qq=|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}}) - Shikinaisha. Famous for the [[:en:Sessho-seki|Sessho-seki]]. The "Nasu Yumoto Daijingū" mentioned in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]] is said to refer to this shrine. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja|温泉神社 (日光市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], [[:en:Kinugawa Onsen|Kinugawa Onsen]]) * {{ill|Yumae Shrine|ja|湯前神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumae Shrine|qq=|1=Yuzen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563474}} ([[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Atami|Atami]], [[:en:Atami Onsen|Atami Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Izuyama Shrine|ja|射山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuyama Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]], {{ill|Sakakibara Onsen|ja|榊原温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakibara Onsen|qq=|1=Sakakibara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541719}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|ja|湯泉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|qq=|1=Yusen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563671}} ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Kobe)]], [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Sometimes referred to as "Onsen Shrine." Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and Kumano-Kusumi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture, [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka]], {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}) - Enshrines Yuyama-no-Kami (湯山主神) and others. It is said that Dōchi, who visited this shrine, was divinely inspired to open the public bath "Mandarayu." * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|温泉神社 (白浜町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District]], [[:en:Shirahama, Wakayama|Shirahama]], [[:en:Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|Nanki Shirahama Onsen]]) * {{ill|Yumoto Shrine|ja|湯本神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Maniwa|Maniwa]], {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}) - The location was formerly called Yubara, but upon Maniwa City's establishment, it was renamed Yubara Onsen. * {{ill|Miyu Shrine|ja|御湯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwami District, Tottori|Iwami District]], [[:en:Iwami, Tottori|Iwami]], {{ill|Iwai Onsen|ja|岩井温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Onsen|qq=|1=Iwai Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474068}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Yakami-hime-no-Mikoto, Miino-Kami, and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Shrine|ja|玉作湯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukuri Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Tamatsukuri Onsen|Tamatsukuri Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Kushiakarutama-no-Kami, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|ja|湯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Higashidōgo Shrine|ja|東道後神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日尾八幡神社}} (Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama, {{ill|Higashidōgo Onsen|ja|東道後温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Onsen|qq=|1=Higashidōgo Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528419}}) - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hio Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Hio Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505266}}. Enshrines Amenominakanushi-no-Kami, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, and Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]]) - Founded during the Taishō period. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. Currently merged into {{ill|Hachiman Asami Shrine|ja|八幡朝見神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Asami Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, {{ill|Tetsuwana Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuwana Onsen|qq=|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}}) * {{ill|Hinokuma Hime Shrine|ja|火男火売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokuma Hime Shrine|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, Tetsuwana Onsen) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Hinokagutsuchi-no-Mikoto and Hiyakehayahime-no-Mikoto. It is said that [[:en:Ippen|Ippen]] Shōnin was inspired by this shrine, called Tsurumi Gongen, to create a stone steam bath (mushi-yu). "Hayami no Yu," a hot spring flowing from the base of the sacred Tsurumidake mountain, is believed to have been directed to Dōgo Onsen by Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto through an underwater channel. * {{ill|Unzen Onsen Shrine|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unzen Onsen Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Unzen, Nagasaki|Unzen]], {{ill|Obama Onsen|ja|小浜町雲仙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obama Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Unzen Onsen|Unzen Onsen]]) - A Kokusai-mizai-sha. Dedicated to the deity of Tsukushi Island (modern [[:en:Kyushu|Kyūshū]]), represented in the form of "Isshin Shimen" (a single body with four faces). The shrine is also known as "Shimen-gū." The name "Unzen" derives from the reading of 温泉 (onsen), but the shrine is pronounced as written: "Onsen Jinja." Over a dozen branch shrines of the same name exist within the [[:en:Shimabara Peninsula|Shimabara Peninsula]]. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja|温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, [[:en:Shimabara, Nagasaki|Shimabara]], Kamenokō-chō) * {{ill|Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja|温泉熊野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Kumano Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Shimabara) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Kunimi-machi Tabirachō) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Azuma-machi Fuenami) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Chijiwa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, [[:en:Minamishimabara|Minamishimabara]], Ariake-machi Yamakawa) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Minamishimabara, Kazusa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja|温泉神社 (八代市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Yatsushiro|Yatsushiro]], {{ill|Hinagu Onsen|ja|日奈久温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinagu Onsen|qq=|1=Hinagu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505236}}) - Enshrines Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto. == See Also == * revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11562963]] Wikidata name: onsen shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Onsen Shrine (disambiguation)}} '''Onsen Shrine''' (温泉神社, Onsen Jinja) refers to a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]], or a commonly used name, associated with [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] (hot springs).
== Overview == An Onsen Shrine is a shrine connected to or dedicated to hot springs. Such shrines are found across Japan, primarily in hot spring areas.
Japan's landscape, intersected by volcanic zones, is abundant in hot springs. Since ancient times, hot springs have been revered as the abodes of deities, known as "Hot Spring Deities" or "Onsen Kami." Shrines dedicated to these deities are referred to as "Yugami Shrines" or "Onsen Shrines." Many ancient hot springs are traditionally associated with deities such as [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] and {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, who are often enshrined as Onsen Kami.
According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, there are three shrines specifically named "Onsen Shrine," along with others such as "Onsen Stone Shrine," "Hot Spring Shrine," "Yugami Shrine," "Miyu Shrine," and "Tamakusuyugami Shrine," making a total of ten shrines related to hot springs listed as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}.
== Shrines Related to Hot Springs Across Japan ==
* {{ill|Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja|温泉石神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]], {{ill|Kawatabi Onsen|ja|川渡温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawatabi Onsen|1=Kawatabi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478932}}) - {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja|温泉神社 (大崎市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture, Ōsaki, {{ill|Naruko Onsen|ja|鳴子温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruko Onsen|1=Naruko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675321}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yuzusame Shrine|ja|由豆佐売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzusame Shrine|1=Yuzusame Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577650}} ([[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], {{ill|Yutagawa Onsen|ja|湯田川温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutagawa Onsen|1=Yutagawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563759}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Mizokuihime-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|湯泉神社 (郡山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōriyama|Kōriyama]], {{ill|Bandai-Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandai-Atami Onsen|1=Bandai Atami Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587893}}) - Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, among others. Divinity of hot springs brought from Izu by the {{ill|Azumi Itō Clan|ja|安積伊東氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Itō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]], [[:en:Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja|温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], {{ill|Kurobane Onsen|ja|黒羽温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurobane Onsen|1=Kurobane Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678785}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja|温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara]], {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Nasu Onsen Shrine|ja|那須温泉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen Shrine|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District]], [[:en:Nasu, Tochigi|Nasu]], {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}}) - Shikinaisha. Famous for the [[:en:Sessho-seki|Sessho-seki]]. The "Nasu Yumoto Daijingū" mentioned in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]] is said to refer to this shrine. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja|温泉神社 (日光市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], [[:en:Kinugawa Onsen|Kinugawa Onsen]]) * {{ill|Yumae Shrine|ja|湯前神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumae Shrine|1=Yuzen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563474}} ([[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Atami|Atami]], [[:en:Atami Onsen|Atami Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Izuyama Shrine|ja|射山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]], {{ill|Sakakibara Onsen|ja|榊原温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakibara Onsen|1=Sakakibara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541719}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|ja|湯泉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|1=Yusen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563671}} ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Kobe)]], [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Sometimes referred to as "Onsen Shrine." Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and Kumano-Kusumi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture, [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka]], {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}) - Enshrines Yuyama-no-Kami (湯山主神) and others. It is said that Dōchi, who visited this shrine, was divinely inspired to open the public bath "Mandarayu." * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|温泉神社 (白浜町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District]], [[:en:Shirahama, Wakayama|Shirahama]], [[:en:Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|Nanki Shirahama Onsen]]) * {{ill|Yumoto Shrine|ja|湯本神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Maniwa|Maniwa]], {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}) - The location was formerly called Yubara, but upon Maniwa City's establishment, it was renamed Yubara Onsen. * {{ill|Miyu Shrine|ja|御湯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwami District, Tottori|Iwami District]], [[:en:Iwami, Tottori|Iwami]], {{ill|Iwai Onsen|ja|岩井温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Onsen|1=Iwai Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474068}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Yakami-hime-no-Mikoto, Miino-Kami, and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Shrine|ja|玉作湯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukuri Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Tamatsukuri Onsen|Tamatsukuri Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Kushiakarutama-no-Kami, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|ja|湯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Higashidōgo Shrine|ja|東道後神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日尾八幡神社}} (Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama, {{ill|Higashidōgo Onsen|ja|東道後温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Onsen|1=Higashidōgo Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528419}}) - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hio Hachiman Shrine|1=Hio Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505266}}. Enshrines Amenominakanushi-no-Kami, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, and Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]]) - Founded during the Taishō period. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. Currently merged into {{ill|Hachiman Asami Shrine|ja|八幡朝見神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Asami Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, {{ill|Tetsuwana Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuwana Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}}) * {{ill|Hinokuma Hime Shrine|ja|火男火売神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokuma Hime Shrine|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, Tetsuwana Onsen) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Hinokagutsuchi-no-Mikoto and Hiyakehayahime-no-Mikoto. It is said that [[:en:Ippen|Ippen]] Shōnin was inspired by this shrine, called Tsurumi Gongen, to create a stone steam bath (mushi-yu). "Hayami no Yu," a hot spring flowing from the base of the sacred Tsurumidake mountain, is believed to have been directed to Dōgo Onsen by Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto through an underwater channel. * {{ill|Unzen Onsen Shrine|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unzen Onsen Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Unzen, Nagasaki|Unzen]], {{ill|Obama Onsen|ja|小浜町雲仙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obama Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Unzen Onsen|Unzen Onsen]]) - A Kokusai-mizai-sha. Dedicated to the deity of Tsukushi Island (modern [[:en:Kyushu|Kyūshū]]), represented in the form of "Isshin Shimen" (a single body with four faces). The shrine is also known as "Shimen-gū." The name "Unzen" derives from the reading of 温泉 (onsen), but the shrine is pronounced as written: "Onsen Jinja." Over a dozen branch shrines of the same name exist within the [[:en:Shimabara Peninsula|Shimabara Peninsula]]. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja|温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, [[:en:Shimabara, Nagasaki|Shimabara]], Kamenokō-chō) * {{ill|Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja|温泉熊野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Shimabara) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Kunimi-machi Tabirachō) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Azuma-machi Fuenami) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Chijiwa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, [[:en:Minamishimabara|Minamishimabara]], Ariake-machi Yamakawa) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Minamishimabara, Kazusa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja|温泉神社 (八代市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Yatsushiro|Yatsushiro]], {{ill|Hinagu Onsen|ja|日奈久温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinagu Onsen|1=Hinagu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505236}}) - Enshrines Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|Onsen Temple|ja|温泉寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}
