revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]]
{{Otheruses|Onsen Shrine (disambiguation)}} '''Onsen Shrine''' (温泉神社, Onsen Jinja) refers to a type of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}}, or a commonly used name, associated with {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} (hot springs).
== Overview == An Onsen Shrine is a shrine connected to or dedicated to hot springs. Such shrines are found across Japan, primarily in hot spring areas.
Japan's landscape, intersected by volcanic zones, is abundant in hot springs. Since ancient times, hot springs have been revered as the abodes of deities, known as "Hot Spring Deities" or "Onsen Kami." Shrines dedicated to these deities are referred to as "Yugami Shrines" or "Onsen Shrines." Many ancient hot springs are traditionally associated with deities such as {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, who are often enshrined as Onsen Kami.
According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, there are three shrines specifically named "Onsen Shrine," along with others such as "Onsen Stone Shrine," "Hot Spring Shrine," "Yugami Shrine," "Miyu Shrine," and "Tamakusuyugami Shrine," making a total of ten shrines related to hot springs listed as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}.
== Shrines Related to Hot Springs Across Japan ==
* {{ill|Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja|温泉石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|WD=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki}}, {{ill|Kawatabi Onsen|ja|川渡温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawatabi Onsen|1=Kawatabi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478932}}) - {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja|温泉神社 (大崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture, Ōsaki, {{ill|Naruko Onsen|ja|鳴子温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruko Onsen|1=Naruko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675321}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yuzusame Shrine|ja|由豆佐売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzusame Shrine|1=Yuzusame Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577650}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka|en|Tsuruoka|WD=Q734416|lt=Tsuruoka}}, {{ill|Yutagawa Onsen|ja|湯田川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutagawa Onsen|1=Yutagawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563759}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Mizokuihime-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|湯泉神社 (郡山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōriyama|en|Kōriyama|WD=Q264206|lt=Kōriyama}}, {{ill|Bandai-Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandai-Atami Onsen|1=Bandai Atami Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587893}}) - Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, among others. Divinity of hot springs brought from Izu by the {{ill|Azumi Itō Clan|ja|安積伊東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Itō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki}}, {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|en|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|WD=Q11260179|lt=Iwaki Yumoto Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja|温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|WD=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Kurobane Onsen|ja|黒羽温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurobane Onsen|1=Kurobane Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678785}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja|温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara}}, {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Nasu Onsen Shrine|ja|那須温泉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen Shrine|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu}}, {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}}) - Shikinaisha. Famous for the {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sessho-seki}}. The "Nasu Yumoto Daijingū" mentioned in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari}} is said to refer to this shrine. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja|温泉神社 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|WD=Q235753|lt=Nikkō}}, {{ill|Kinugawa Onsen|en|Kinugawa Onsen|WD=Q5363719|lt=Kinugawa Onsen}}) * {{ill|Yumae Shrine|ja|湯前神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumae Shrine|1=Yuzen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563474}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Atami|en|Atami|WD=Q653364|lt=Atami}}, {{ill|Atami Onsen|en|Atami Onsen|WD=Q11569090|lt=Atami Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Izuyama Shrine|ja|射山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu}}, {{ill|Sakakibara Onsen|ja|榊原温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakibara Onsen|1=Sakakibara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541719}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|ja|湯泉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|1=Yusen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563671}} ({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)}}, {{ill|Arima Onsen|en|Arima Onsen|WD=Q4790673|lt=Arima Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Sometimes referred to as "Onsen Shrine." Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and Kumano-Kusumi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|WD=Q696388|lt=Toyooka}}, {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}) - Enshrines Yuyama-no-Kami (湯山主神) and others. It is said that Dōchi, who visited this shrine, was divinely inspired to open the public bath "Mandarayu." * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|温泉神社 (白浜町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District}}, {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|en|Shirahama, Wakayama|WD=Q1202728|lt=Shirahama}}, {{ill|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|en|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|WD=Q11408352|lt=Nanki Shirahama Onsen}}) * {{ill|Yumoto Shrine|ja|湯本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maniwa|en|Maniwa|WD=Q861230|lt=Maniwa}}, {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}) - The location was formerly called Yubara, but upon Maniwa City's establishment, it was renamed Yubara Onsen. * {{ill|Miyu Shrine|ja|御湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=Iwami District}}, {{ill|Iwami, Tottori|en|Iwami, Tottori|WD=Q281925|lt=Iwami}}, {{ill|Iwai Onsen|ja|岩井温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Onsen|1=Iwai Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474068}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Yakami-hime-no-Mikoto, Miino-Kami, and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Shrine|ja|玉作湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukuri Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue}}, {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|en|Tamatsukuri Onsen|WD=Q3514711|lt=Tamatsukuri Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Kushiakarutama-no-Kami, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|ja|湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|qq=}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|WD=Q1037748|lt=Dōgo Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Higashidōgo Shrine|ja|東道後神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日尾八幡神社}} (Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama, {{ill|Higashidōgo Onsen|ja|東道後温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Onsen|1=Higashidōgo Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528419}}) - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hio Hachiman Shrine|1=Hio Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505266}}. Enshrines Amenominakanushi-no-Kami, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, and Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|WD=Q273880|lt=Beppu}}, {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en|Beppu Onsen|WD=Q4890932|lt=Beppu Onsen}}) - Founded during the Taishō period. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. Currently merged into {{ill|Hachiman Asami Shrine|ja|八幡朝見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Asami Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, {{ill|Tetsuwana Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuwana Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}}) * {{ill|Honoo-Honome Shrine|ja|火男火売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoo-Honome Shrine|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, Tetsuwana Onsen) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Hinokagutsuchi-no-Mikoto and Hiyakehayahime-no-Mikoto. It is said that {{ill|Ippen|en|Ippen|WD=Q1056232|lt=Ippen}} Shōnin was inspired by this shrine, called Tsurumi Gongen, to create a stone steam bath (mushi-yu). "Hayami no Yu," a hot spring flowing from the base of the sacred Tsurumidake mountain, is believed to have been directed to Dōgo Onsen by Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto through an underwater channel. * {{ill|Unzen Onsen Shrine|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unzen Onsen Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Unzen, Nagasaki|en|Unzen, Nagasaki|WD=Q999453|lt=Unzen}}, {{ill|Obama Onsen|ja|小浜町雲仙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obama Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Unzen Onsen|en|Unzen Onsen|WD=Q11660005|lt=Unzen Onsen}}) - A Kokusai-mizai-sha. Dedicated to the deity of Tsukushi Island (modern {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyūshū}}), represented in the form of "Isshin Shimen" (a single body with four faces). The shrine is also known as "Shimen-gū." The name "Unzen" derives from the reading of 温泉 (onsen), but the shrine is pronounced as written: "Onsen Jinja." Over a dozen branch shrines of the same name exist within the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|en|Shimabara Peninsula|WD=Q522605|lt=Shimabara Peninsula}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja|温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Shimabara, Nagasaki|en|Shimabara, Nagasaki|WD=Q847594|lt=Shimabara}}, Kamenokō-chō) * {{ill|Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja|温泉熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Shimabara) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Kunimi-machi Tabirachō) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Azuma-machi Fuenami) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Chijiwa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Minamishimabara|en|Minamishimabara|WD=Q990376|lt=Minamishimabara}}, Ariake-machi Yamakawa) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Minamishimabara, Kazusa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja|温泉神社 (八代市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yatsushiro|en|Yatsushiro|WD=Q734479|lt=Yatsushiro}}, {{ill|Hinagu Onsen|ja|日奈久温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinagu Onsen|1=Hinagu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505236}}) - Enshrines Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|Onsen Temple|ja|温泉寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]]
{{Otheruses|Onsen Shrine (disambiguation)}} '''Onsen Shrine''' (温泉神社, Onsen Jinja) refers to a type of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, or a commonly used name, associated with {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} (hot springs).
== Overview == An Onsen Shrine is a shrine connected to or dedicated to hot springs. Such shrines are found across Japan, primarily in hot spring areas.
Japan's landscape, intersected by volcanic zones, is abundant in hot springs. Since ancient times, hot springs have been revered as the abodes of deities, known as "Hot Spring Deities" or "Onsen Kami." Shrines dedicated to these deities are referred to as "Yugami Shrines" or "Onsen Shrines." Many ancient hot springs are traditionally associated with deities such as {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, who are often enshrined as Onsen Kami.
According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, there are three shrines specifically named "Onsen Shrine," along with others such as "Onsen Stone Shrine," "Hot Spring Shrine," "Yugami Shrine," "Miyu Shrine," and "Tamakusuyugami Shrine," making a total of ten shrines related to hot springs listed as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}.
== Shrines Related to Hot Springs Across Japan ==
* {{ill|Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja|温泉石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|WD=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Kawatabi Onsen|ja|川渡温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawatabi Onsen|1=Kawatabi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478932}}) - {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja|温泉神社 (大崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture, Ōsaki, {{ill|Naruko Onsen|ja|鳴子温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruko Onsen|1=Naruko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675321}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yuzusame Shrine|ja|由豆佐売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzusame Shrine|1=Yuzusame Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577650}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka|en|Tsuruoka|WD=Q734416|lt=Tsuruoka|1=Tsuruoka}}, {{ill|Yutagawa Onsen|ja|湯田川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutagawa Onsen|1=Q11563759|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563759}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Mizokuihime-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|湯泉神社 (郡山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōriyama|en|Kōriyama|WD=Q264206|lt=Kōriyama|1=Kōriyama}}, {{ill|Bandai-Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandai-Atami Onsen|1=Bandai Atami Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587893}}) - Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, among others. Divinity of hot springs brought from Izu by the {{ill|Azumi Itō Clan|ja|安積伊東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Itō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|en|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|WD=Q11260179|lt=Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|1=Iwaki Yumoto Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja|温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|WD=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara|1=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Kurobane Onsen|ja|黒羽温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurobane Onsen|1=Q11678785|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678785}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja|温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara|1=Nasushiobara}}, {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Nasu Onsen Shrine|ja|那須温泉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen Shrine|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu|1=Nasu, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}}) - Shikinaisha. Famous for the {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sessho-seki|1=Sessho-seki}}. The "Nasu Yumoto Daijingū" mentioned in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari|1=The Tale of the Heike}} is said to refer to this shrine. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja|温泉神社 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|WD=Q235753|lt=Nikkō|1=Nikkō}}, {{ill|Kinugawa Onsen|en|Kinugawa Onsen|WD=Q5363719|lt=Kinugawa Onsen|1=Kinugawa Onsen}}) * {{ill|Yumae Shrine|ja|湯前神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumae Shrine|1=Yuzen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563474}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Atami|en|Atami|WD=Q653364|lt=Atami|1=Atami}}, {{ill|Atami Onsen|en|Atami Onsen|WD=Q11569090|lt=Atami Onsen|1=Atami Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Izuyama Shrine|ja|射山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu|1=Tsu, Mie}}, {{ill|Sakakibara Onsen|ja|榊原温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakibara Onsen|1=Q11541719|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541719}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|ja|湯泉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|1=Yusen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563671}} ({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|1=北區}}, {{ill|Arima Onsen|en|Arima Onsen|WD=Q4790673|lt=Arima Onsen|1=Arima Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Sometimes referred to as "Onsen Shrine." Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and Kumano-Kusumi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|WD=Q696388|lt=Toyooka|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}) - Enshrines Yuyama-no-Kami (湯山主神) and others. It is said that Dōchi, who visited this shrine, was divinely inspired to open the public bath "Mandarayu." * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|温泉神社 (白浜町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District|1=Nishimuro District, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|en|Shirahama, Wakayama|WD=Q1202728|lt=Shirahama|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|en|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|WD=Q11408352|lt=Nanki Shirahama Onsen|1=Nanki-Shirahama Onsen}}) * {{ill|Yumoto Shrine|ja|湯本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maniwa|en|Maniwa|WD=Q861230|lt=Maniwa|1=Maniwa}}, {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}) - The location was formerly called Yubara, but upon Maniwa City's establishment, it was renamed Yubara Onsen. * {{ill|Miyu Shrine|ja|御湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=Iwami District|1=Iwami District, Tottori}}, {{ill|Iwami, Tottori|en|Iwami, Tottori|WD=Q281925|lt=Iwami|1=Iwami, Tottori}}, {{ill|Iwai Onsen|ja|岩井温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Onsen|1=Iwai Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474068}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Yakami-hime-no-Mikoto, Miino-Kami, and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Shrine|ja|玉作湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukuri Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|en|Tamatsukuri Onsen|WD=Q3514711|lt=Tamatsukuri Onsen|1=Tamatsukuri Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Kushiakarutama-no-Kami, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|ja|湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|qq=}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|WD=Q1037748|lt=Dōgo Onsen|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Higashidōgo Shrine|ja|東道後神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日尾八幡神社}} (Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama, {{ill|Higashidōgo Onsen|ja|東道後温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Onsen|1=Q11528419|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528419}}) - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hio Hachiman Shrine|1=Higashidōgo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505266}}. Enshrines Amenominakanushi-no-Kami, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, and Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|WD=Q273880|lt=Beppu|1=Beppu}}, {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en|Beppu Onsen|WD=Q4890932|lt=Beppu Onsen|1=Beppu Onsen}}) - Founded during the Taishō period. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. Currently merged into {{ill|Hachiman Asami Shrine|ja|八幡朝見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Asami Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, {{ill|Tetsuwana Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuwana Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}}) * {{ill|Honoo-Honome Shrine|ja|火男火売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoo-Honome Shrine|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, Tetsuwana Onsen) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Hinokagutsuchi-no-Mikoto and Hiyakehayahime-no-Mikoto. It is said that {{ill|Ippen|en|Ippen|WD=Q1056232|lt=Ippen|1=Ippen}} Shōnin was inspired by this shrine, called Tsurumi Gongen, to create a stone steam bath (mushi-yu). "Hayami no Yu," a hot spring flowing from the base of the sacred Tsurumidake mountain, is believed to have been directed to Dōgo Onsen by Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto through an underwater channel. * {{ill|Unzen Onsen Shrine|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unzen Onsen Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Unzen, Nagasaki|en|Unzen, Nagasaki|WD=Q999453|lt=Unzen|1=Unzen, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Obama Onsen|ja|小浜町雲仙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obama Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Unzen Onsen|en|Unzen Onsen|WD=Q11660005|lt=Unzen Onsen|1=Unzen Onsen}}) - A Kokusai-mizai-sha. Dedicated to the deity of Tsukushi Island (modern {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyūshū|1=Kyushu}}), represented in the form of "Isshin Shimen" (a single body with four faces). The shrine is also known as "Shimen-gū." The name "Unzen" derives from the reading of 温泉 (onsen), but the shrine is pronounced as written: "Onsen Jinja." Over a dozen branch shrines of the same name exist within the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|en|Shimabara Peninsula|WD=Q522605|lt=Shimabara Peninsula|1=Shimabara Peninsula}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja|温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Shimabara, Nagasaki|en|Shimabara, Nagasaki|WD=Q847594|lt=Shimabara|1=Shimabara, Nagasaki}}, Kamenokō-chō) * {{ill|Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja|温泉熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Shimabara) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Kunimi-machi Tabirachō) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Azuma-machi Fuenami) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Chijiwa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Minamishimabara|en|Minamishimabara|WD=Q990376|lt=Minamishimabara|1=Minamishimabara}}, Ariake-machi Yamakawa) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Minamishimabara, Kazusa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja|温泉神社 (八代市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yatsushiro|en|Yatsushiro|WD=Q734479|lt=Yatsushiro|1=Yatsushiro}}, {{ill|Hinagu Onsen|ja|日奈久温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinagu Onsen|1=Q11505236|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505236}}) - Enshrines Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|Onsen Temple|ja|温泉寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]]
{{Otheruses|Onsen Shrine (disambiguation)}} '''Onsen Shrine''' (温泉神社, Onsen Jinja) refers to a type of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, or a commonly used name, associated with {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} (hot springs).
== Overview == An Onsen Shrine is a shrine connected to or dedicated to hot springs. Such shrines are found across Japan, primarily in hot spring areas.
Japan's landscape, intersected by volcanic zones, is abundant in hot springs. Since ancient times, hot springs have been revered as the abodes of deities, known as "Hot Spring Deities" or "Onsen Kami." Shrines dedicated to these deities are referred to as "Yugami Shrines" or "Onsen Shrines." Many ancient hot springs are traditionally associated with deities such as {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, who are often enshrined as Onsen Kami.
According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, there are three shrines specifically named "Onsen Shrine," along with others such as "Onsen Stone Shrine," "Hot Spring Shrine," "Yugami Shrine," "Miyu Shrine," and "Tamakusuyugami Shrine," making a total of ten shrines related to hot springs listed as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}.
== Shrines Related to Hot Springs Across Japan ==
* {{ill|Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja|温泉石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|WD=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Kawatabi Onsen|ja|川渡温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawatabi Onsen|1=Kawatabi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478932}}) - {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja|温泉神社 (大崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture, Ōsaki, {{ill|Naruko Onsen|ja|鳴子温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruko Onsen|1=Naruko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675321}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yuzusame Shrine|ja|由豆佐売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzusame Shrine|1=Yuzusame Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577650}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka|en|Tsuruoka|WD=Q734416|lt=Tsuruoka|1=Tsuruoka}}, {{ill|Yutagawa Onsen|ja|湯田川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutagawa Onsen|1=Q11563759|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563759}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Mizokuihime-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|湯泉神社 (郡山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōriyama|en|Kōriyama|WD=Q264206|lt=Kōriyama|1=Kōriyama}}, {{ill|Bandai-Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandai-Atami Onsen|1=Bandai Atami Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587893}}) - Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, among others. Divinity of hot springs brought from Izu by the {{ill|Azumi Itō Clan|ja|安積伊東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Itō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|en|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|WD=Q11260179|lt=Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|1=Iwaki Yumoto Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja|温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|WD=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara|1=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Kurobane Onsen|ja|黒羽温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurobane Onsen|1=Q11678785|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678785}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja|温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara|1=Nasushiobara}}, {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Nasu Onsen Shrine|ja|那須温泉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen Shrine|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu|1=Nasu, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}}) - Shikinaisha. Famous for the {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sessho-seki|1=Sessho-seki}}. The "Nasu Yumoto Daijingū" mentioned in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari|1=The Tale of the Heike}} is said to refer to this shrine. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja|温泉神社 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|WD=Q235753|lt=Nikkō|1=Nikkō}}, {{ill|Kinugawa Onsen|en|Kinugawa Onsen|WD=Q5363719|lt=Kinugawa Onsen|1=Kinugawa Onsen}}) * {{ill|Yumae Shrine|ja|湯前神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumae Shrine|1=Yuzen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563474}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Atami|en|Atami|WD=Q653364|lt=Atami|1=Atami}}, {{ill|Atami Onsen|en|Atami Onsen|WD=Q11569090|lt=Atami Onsen|1=Atami Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Izuyama Shrine|ja|射山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu|1=Tsu, Mie}}, {{ill|Sakakibara Onsen|ja|榊原温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakibara Onsen|1=Q11541719|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541719}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|ja|湯泉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|1=Yusen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563671}} ({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|1=北區}}, {{ill|Arima Onsen|en|Arima Onsen|WD=Q4790673|lt=Arima Onsen|1=Arima Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Sometimes referred to as "Onsen Shrine." Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and Kumano-Kusumi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|WD=Q696388|lt=Toyooka|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}) - Enshrines Yuyama-no-Kami (湯山主神) and others. It is said that Dōchi, who visited this shrine, was divinely inspired to open the public bath "Mandarayu." * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|温泉神社 (白浜町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District|1=Nishimuro District, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|en|Shirahama, Wakayama|WD=Q1202728|lt=Shirahama|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|en|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|WD=Q11408352|lt=Nanki Shirahama Onsen|1=Nanki-Shirahama Onsen}}) * {{ill|Yumoto Shrine|ja|湯本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maniwa|en|Maniwa|WD=Q861230|lt=Maniwa|1=Maniwa}}, {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}) - The location was formerly called Yubara, but upon Maniwa City's establishment, it was renamed Yubara Onsen. * {{ill|Miyu Shrine|ja|御湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=Iwami District|1=Iwami District, Tottori}}, {{ill|Iwami, Tottori|en|Iwami, Tottori|WD=Q281925|lt=Iwami|1=Iwami, Tottori}}, {{ill|Iwai Onsen|ja|岩井温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Onsen|1=Iwai Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474068}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Yakami-hime-no-Mikoto, Miino-Kami, and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Shrine|ja|玉作湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukuri Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|en|Tamatsukuri Onsen|WD=Q3514711|lt=Tamatsukuri Onsen|1=Tamatsukuri Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Kushiakarutama-no-Kami, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|ja|湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|qq=}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|WD=Q1037748|lt=Dōgo Onsen|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Higashidōgo Shrine|ja|東道後神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日尾八幡神社}} (Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama, {{ill|Higashidōgo Onsen|ja|東道後温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Onsen|1=Q11528419|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528419}}) - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hio Hachiman Shrine|1=Higashidōgo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505266}}. Enshrines Amenominakanushi-no-Kami, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, and Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|WD=Q273880|lt=Beppu|1=Beppu}}, {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en|Beppu Onsen|WD=Q4890932|lt=Beppu Onsen|1=Beppu Onsen}}) - Founded during the Taishō period. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. Currently merged into {{ill|Hachiman Asami Shrine|ja|八幡朝見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Asami Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, {{ill|Tetsuwana Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuwana Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}}) * {{ill|Honoo-Honome Shrine|ja|火男火売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoo-Honome Shrine|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, Tetsuwana Onsen) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Hinokagutsuchi-no-Mikoto and Hiyakehayahime-no-Mikoto. It is said that {{ill|Ippen|en|Ippen|WD=Q1056232|lt=Ippen|1=Ippen}} Shōnin was inspired by this shrine, called Tsurumi Gongen, to create a stone steam bath (mushi-yu). "Hayami no Yu," a hot spring flowing from the base of the sacred Tsurumidake mountain, is believed to have been directed to Dōgo Onsen by Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto through an underwater channel. * {{ill|Unzen Onsen Shrine|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unzen Onsen Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Unzen, Nagasaki|en|Unzen, Nagasaki|WD=Q999453|lt=Unzen|1=Unzen, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Obama Onsen|ja|小浜町雲仙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obama Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Unzen Onsen|en|Unzen Onsen|WD=Q11660005|lt=Unzen Onsen|1=Unzen Onsen}}) - A Kokusai-mizai-sha. Dedicated to the deity of Tsukushi Island (modern {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyūshū|1=Kyushu}}), represented in the form of "Isshin Shimen" (a single body with four faces). The shrine is also known as "Shimen-gū." The name "Unzen" derives from the reading of 温泉 (onsen), but the shrine is pronounced as written: "Onsen Jinja." Over a dozen branch shrines of the same name exist within the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|en|Shimabara Peninsula|WD=Q522605|lt=Shimabara Peninsula|1=Shimabara Peninsula}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja|温泉神社 (島原市亀の甲町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shimabara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Shimabara, Nagasaki|en|Shimabara, Nagasaki|WD=Q847594|lt=Shimabara|1=Shimabara, Nagasaki}}, Kamenokō-chō) * {{ill|Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja|温泉熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Kumano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Shimabara) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市国見町多比良丁)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Kunimi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Kunimi-machi Tabirachō) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市吾妻町布江名)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Azuma)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Azuma-machi Fuenami) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市千々石町己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Unzen Chijiwa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Unzen, Chijiwa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市有家町山川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Ariake)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Minamishimabara|en|Minamishimabara|WD=Q990376|lt=Minamishimabara|1=Minamishimabara}}, Ariake-machi Yamakawa) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja|温泉神社 (南島原市加津佐町己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Minamishimabara Kazusa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagasaki Prefecture, Minamishimabara, Kazusa-machi Mi) * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja|温泉神社 (八代市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Yatsushiro)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yatsushiro|en|Yatsushiro|WD=Q734479|lt=Yatsushiro|1=Yatsushiro}}, {{ill|Hinagu Onsen|ja|日奈久温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinagu Onsen|1=Q11505236|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505236}}) - Enshrines Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|Onsen Temple|ja|温泉寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉神社]]
{{Otheruses|Onsen Shrine (disambiguation)}} '''Onsen Shrine''' (温泉神社, Onsen Jinja) refers to a type of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, or a commonly used name, associated with {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} (hot springs).
== Overview == An Onsen Shrine is a shrine connected to or dedicated to hot springs. Such shrines are found across Japan, primarily in hot spring areas.
Japan's landscape, intersected by volcanic zones, is abundant in hot springs. Since ancient times, hot springs have been revered as the abodes of deities, known as "Hot Spring Deities" or "Onsen Kami." Shrines dedicated to these deities are referred to as "Yugami Shrines" or "Onsen Shrines." Many ancient hot springs are traditionally associated with deities such as {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, who are often enshrined as Onsen Kami.
According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, there are three shrines specifically named "Onsen Shrine," along with others such as "Onsen Stone Shrine," "Hot Spring Shrine," "Yugami Shrine," "Miyu Shrine," and "Tamakusuyugami Shrine," making a total of ten shrines related to hot springs listed as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}.
== Shrines Related to Hot Springs Across Japan ==
* {{ill|Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja|温泉石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Ishi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|WD=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Kawatabi Onsen|ja|川渡温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawatabi Onsen|1=Kawatabi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478932}}) - {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja|温泉神社 (大崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōsaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture, Ōsaki, {{ill|Naruko Onsen|ja|鳴子温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruko Onsen|1=Naruko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675321}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yuzusame Shrine|ja|由豆佐売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzusame Shrine|1=Yuzusame Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577650}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka|en|Tsuruoka|WD=Q734416|lt=Tsuruoka|1=Tsuruoka}}, {{ill|Yutagawa Onsen|ja|湯田川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutagawa Onsen|1=Q11563759|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563759}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Mizokuihime-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|湯泉神社 (郡山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōriyama|en|Kōriyama|WD=Q264206|lt=Kōriyama|1=Kōriyama}}, {{ill|Bandai-Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandai-Atami Onsen|1=Bandai Atami Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587893}}) - Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, among others. Divinity of hot springs brought from Izu by the {{ill|Azumi Itō Clan|ja|安積伊東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Itō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|en|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|WD=Q11260179|lt=Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|1=Iwaki Yumoto Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja|温泉神社 (大田原市中野内)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Ōtawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|WD=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara|1=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Kurobane Onsen|ja|黒羽温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurobane Onsen|1=Q11678785|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678785}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja|温泉神社 (那須塩原市湯本塩原)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nasushiobara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara|1=Nasushiobara}}, {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}) - Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Nasu Onsen Shrine|ja|那須温泉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen Shrine|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu|1=Nasu, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}}) - Shikinaisha. Famous for the {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sessho-seki|1=Sessho-seki}}. The "Nasu Yumoto Daijingū" mentioned in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari|1=The Tale of the Heike}} is said to refer to this shrine. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja|温泉神社 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|WD=Q235753|lt=Nikkō|1=Nikkō}}, {{ill|Kinugawa Onsen|en|Kinugawa Onsen|WD=Q5363719|lt=Kinugawa Onsen|1=Kinugawa Onsen}}) * {{ill|Yumae Shrine|ja|湯前神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumae Shrine|1=Yuzen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563474}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Atami|en|Atami|WD=Q653364|lt=Atami|1=Atami}}, {{ill|Atami Onsen|en|Atami Onsen|WD=Q11569090|lt=Atami Onsen|1=Atami Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Izuyama Shrine|ja|射山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu|1=Tsu, Mie}}, {{ill|Sakakibara Onsen|ja|榊原温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakibara Onsen|1=Q11541719|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541719}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|ja|湯泉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusen Shrine (Kobe)|1=Yusen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563671}} ({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|1=北區}}, {{ill|Arima Onsen|en|Arima Onsen|WD=Q4790673|lt=Arima Onsen|1=Arima Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Sometimes referred to as "Onsen Shrine." Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and Kumano-Kusumi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|WD=Q696388|lt=Toyooka|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}) - Enshrines Yuyama-no-Kami (湯山主神) and others. It is said that Dōchi, who visited this shrine, was divinely inspired to open the public bath "Mandarayu." * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|温泉神社 (白浜町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Shirahama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District|1=Nishimuro District, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|en|Shirahama, Wakayama|WD=Q1202728|lt=Shirahama|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|en|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|WD=Q11408352|lt=Nanki Shirahama Onsen|1=Nanki-Shirahama Onsen}}) * {{ill|Yumoto Shrine|ja|湯本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maniwa|en|Maniwa|WD=Q861230|lt=Maniwa|1=Maniwa}}, {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}) - The location was formerly called Yubara, but upon Maniwa City's establishment, it was renamed Yubara Onsen. * {{ill|Miyu Shrine|ja|御湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=Iwami District|1=Iwami District, Tottori}}, {{ill|Iwami, Tottori|en|Iwami, Tottori|WD=Q281925|lt=Iwami|1=Iwami, Tottori}}, {{ill|Iwai Onsen|ja|岩井温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Onsen|1=Iwai Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474068}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Yakami-hime-no-Mikoto, Miino-Kami, and Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Tamatsukuri Shrine|ja|玉作湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukuri Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|en|Tamatsukuri Onsen|WD=Q3514711|lt=Tamatsukuri Onsen|1=Tamatsukuri Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Kushiakarutama-no-Kami, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine|lt=Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|ja|湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yu Shrine (Dōgo Onsen)|qq=}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|WD=Q1037748|lt=Dōgo Onsen|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Higashidōgo Shrine|ja|東道後神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日尾八幡神社}} (Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama, {{ill|Higashidōgo Onsen|ja|東道後温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashidōgo Onsen|1=Q11528419|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528419}}) - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hio Hachiman Shrine|1=Higashidōgo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505266}}. Enshrines Amenominakanushi-no-Kami, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, and Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市青山町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu)|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|WD=Q273880|lt=Beppu|1=Beppu}}, {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en|Beppu Onsen|WD=Q4890932|lt=Beppu Onsen|1=Beppu Onsen}}) - Founded during the Taishō period. Enshrines Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto, and others. Currently merged into {{ill|Hachiman Asami Shrine|ja|八幡朝見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Asami Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|ja|温泉神社 (別府市鉄輪)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine (Beppu Tetsuwana)|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, {{ill|Tetsuwana Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuwana Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}}) * {{ill|Honoo-Honome Shrine|ja|火男火売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoo-Honome Shrine|qq=}} (Ōita Prefecture, Beppu, Tetsuwana Onsen) - Shikinaisha. Enshrines Hinokagutsuchi-no-Mikoto and Hiyakehayahime-no-Mikoto. It is said that {{ill|Ippen|en|Ippen|WD=Q1056232|lt=Ippen|1=Ippen}} Shōnin was inspired by this shrine, called Tsurumi Gongen, to create a stone steam bath (mushi-yu). "Hayami no Yu," a hot spring flowing from the base of the sacred Tsurumidake mountain, is believed to have been directed to Dōgo Onsen by Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto through an underwater channel. * {{ill|Unzen Onsen Shrine|ja|温泉神社 (雲仙市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unzen Onsen Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Unzen, Nagasaki|en|Unzen, Nagasaki|WD=Q999453|lt=Unzen|1=Unzen, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Obama Onsen|ja|小浜町雲仙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obama Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Unzen Onsen|en|Unzen Onsen|WD=Q11660005|lt=Unzen Onsen|1=Unzen Onsen}}) - A Kokusai-mizai-sha. Dedicated to the deity of Tsukushi Island (modern {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyūshū|1=Kyushu}}), represented in the form of "Isshin Shimen" (a single body with four faces). The shrine is also known as "Shimen-gū." The name "Unzen" derives from the reading of 温泉 (onsen), but the shrine is pronounced as written: "Onsen Jinja." Over a dozen branch shrines of the same name exist within the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|en|Shimabara Peninsula|WD=Q522605|lt=Shimabara Peninsula|1=Shimabara Peninsula}}. * {{ill|Onsen Shrine
